findings.description: Parallelism contradiction: Overview states 'All pipeline stages run in parallel to maximize throughput' but Stage 2 states 'Stage 2 must complete before Stage 3 begins', which describes sequential dependency.
findings.evidence: Overview: 'All pipeline stages run in parallel to maximize throughput.' Stage 2: 'Stage 2 must complete before Stage 3 begins.'
findings.suggestion: Correct the Overview to describe the actual execution model — staged/sequential with possible parallelism within stages, not all-parallel.

findings.description: Dangling cross-reference: 'Security considerations are documented in Section 6' but no Section 6 exists in the document.
findings.evidence: 'Security considerations are documented in Section 6.' — document contains Overview, Architecture Components, Performance Characteristics, Operational Notes, and Conclusions. No Section 6.
findings.suggestion: Either add a Security Considerations section or remove the dangling reference and incorporate security notes inline.

findings.description: Batch vs streaming performance claim is factually inverted. For an event pipeline handling 50K events/sec, streaming is typically faster for small payloads; batch aggregation is more efficient for large payloads.
findings.evidence: 'Benchmarks show that batch processing is 3x faster than streaming for files under 10MB. For larger payloads, streaming is preferred.'
findings.suggestion: Correct to: streaming is more efficient for small, high-frequency events; batch processing is preferred for large payload aggregations. Alternatively, provide sourced benchmarks if the original claim is intentional.

findings.description: 99.9% latency reduction claim is extraordinary and unsupported. A 99.9% reduction implies going from e.g. 10 seconds to 10 milliseconds, which is implausible from adding an in-memory cache to micro-batch writes.
findings.evidence: 'The in-memory delivery cache reduces end-to-end latency by 99.9% compared to unbuffered writes.'
findings.suggestion: Replace with a defensible figure or describe the improvement qualitatively (e.g., 'significantly reduces latency').

findings.description: 'Standard retry policy' referenced but never defined. Reader has no way to know what this policy entails (max retries, backoff strategy, timeout).
findings.evidence: 'Transformation applies the standard retry policy on transient failures.'
findings.suggestion: Define the retry policy (e.g., 'exponential backoff with up to 3 retries, initial delay 1s') or link to where it is defined.

findings.description: 'Large files' threshold is never defined. Reader cannot determine when the overflow queue is triggered.
findings.evidence: 'Large files are handled separately from the primary transformation path via an overflow queue.'
findings.suggestion: Specify the size threshold (e.g., 'Files exceeding 50MB are routed to the overflow queue').

findings.description: 'Pipeline' and 'workflow' are used interchangeably without distinction. The document uses 'pipeline' as the primary term but introduces 'workflow' in Monitoring and Operational Notes without defining the relationship.
findings.evidence: Monitoring: 'Polling for status updates uses a 10-minute interval.' Operational Notes: 'The pipeline and workflow are monitored by the on-call team.' Conclusions: 'adding a real-time streaming workflow alongside the existing batch pipeline.'
findings.suggestion: Standardize terminology. Use 'pipeline' consistently, or define 'workflow' as a distinct concept if intended.

findings.description: ETL acronym used without expansion on first use.
findings.evidence: '...an internal ETL system that handles batch ingestion...'
findings.suggestion: Expand to 'Extract, Transform, Load (ETL)' on first use.

findings.description: Authentication type not specified. For an architecture document, the authentication mechanism should be named.
findings.evidence: 'Authentication is used to verify each request before processing begins.'
findings.suggestion: Specify the authentication type (e.g., 'API key authentication' or 'OAuth 2.0 bearer token authentication').
